title: Second non-English locale (UI + core explainers), accuracy-reviewed
context: The M4 definition of done requires >= 2 non-English locales (UI + core explainers) accuracy-reviewed. The second locale should be partner-driven, reflecting the priority languages of the communities served. Translation accuracy for health/regulatory meaning is mandatory — a fluent but technically wrong translation of an MCL or violation type can cause the same harms as an inaccurate English explainer. Expert escalation applies to health content translations.
objective: Produce and ship a second non-English locale (partner-determined language) for all UI strings and core explainer templates: (1) building on the i18n framework from wqe-i18n-017; (2) accuracy-reviewed for health/regulatory meaning by a competent speaker, not just fluency; (3) expert escalation for health content; (4) build-time completeness check passes; (5) safe fallback to English enforced; (6) licensed CC-BY-4.0.
acceptanceCriteria: Second non-English locale covers all UI strings and core explainer templates
Build-time completeness check passes with no missing translation keys
Safe fallback to English enforced; no blank or broken health/safety text in the new locale
Translations accuracy-reviewed for health and regulatory meaning by a competent speaker; expert escalation for health content
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: Translation: second non-English locale locale files for UI + core explainers, with accuracy review documented.
